output.code:
# Let's open the file to see contents
with open('/mnt/data/file-8hzRW4JwbwcBPxSDX58nGn-text_to_analyse.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
print(text)

# find all quotes
quotes = [i for i,ch in enumerate(text) if ch=='"']
quotes

# Let's implement extraction
import json

# define punctuation to strip
punct = set(['.', ',', '!', '?', '…', ';', ':', '"', "'", '«', '»', '(', ')', '[', ']', '{', '}', '<', '>', 'ـ'])
# whitespace and newlines etc handled by strip()

# span1 raw
raw1 = text[416:1079]
# trim whitespace
span1 = raw1.strip()
# trim leading punctuation
while len(span1)>0 and (span1[0] in punct or span1[0].isspace() or span1[0]=='\n' or span1[0]=='\t'):
    span1 = span1[1:]
# trim trailing punctuation and whitespace
while len(span1)>0 and (span1[-1] in punct or span1[-1].isspace() or span1[-1]=='\n' or span1[-1]=='\t'):
    span1 = span1[:-1]

# find index
idx1 = text.find(span1)
print("Span1 idx1:", idx1, "len:", len(span1))

# span2 raw
raw2 = text[1362:4317]
span2 = raw2.strip()
# trim leading
while len(span2)>0 and (span2[0] in punct or span2[0].isspace() or span2[0]=='\n' or span2[0]=='\t'):
    span2 = span2[1:]
# trim trailing
while len(span2)>0 and (span2[-1] in punct or span2[-1].isspace() or span2[-1]=='\n' or span2[-1]=='\t'):
    span2 = span2[:-1]

idx2 = text.find(span2)
print("Span2 idx2:", idx2, "len:", len(span2))

# print samples
print("Span1 text:", span1[:50], "...", span1[-50:])
print("Span2 text:", span2[:50], "...", span2[-50:])

assert text[idx1:idx1+len(span1)] == span1
assert text[idx2:idx2+len(span2)] == span2
